title: HRANDFIELD
description: Returns one or more random fields from a hash.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields returned

redis_py.signature: hrandfield(key: str, count: Optional[int] = None, withvalues: bool = False)

returns.description: multiple random fields from a hash.
multiple random fields from a hash.

jedis.signature: List<String> hrandfield(final String key, final long count)

go-redis.signature: HRandField(ctx context.Context, key string, count int)

ioredis.signature: hrandfield(key: RedisKey, count: number | string, callback?: Callback<string | unknown[] | null>)
hrandfield(key: RedisKey, count: number | string, withvalues: "WITHVALUES", callback?: Callback<string | unknown[] | null>)

content:
When called with just the `key` argument, return a random field from the hash value stored at `key`. If the provided `count` argument is positive, return an array of **distinct fields**. The array's length is either `count` or the hash's number of fields ([`HLEN`]()), whichever is lower. If called with a negative `count`, the behavior changes and the command is allowed to return the **same field multiple times**. In this case, the number of returned fields is the absolute value of the specified `count`. The optional `WITHVALUES` modifier changes the reply so it includes the respective values of the randomly selected hash fields. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} HSET coin heads obverse tails reverse edge null HRANDFIELD coin HRANDFIELD coin HRANDFIELD coin -5 WITHVALUES {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Specification of the behavior when count is passed When the `count` argument is a positive value this command behaves as follows: * No repeated fields are returned. * If `count` is bigger than the number of fields in the hash, the command will only return the whole hash without additional fields. * The order of fields in the reply is not truly random, so it is up to the client to shuffle them if needed. When the `count` is a negative value, the behavior changes as follows: * Repeating fields are possible. * Exactly `count` fields, or an empty array if the hash is empty (non-existing key), are always returned. * The order of fields in the reply is truly random. ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="hrandfield-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} Any of the following: * [Nil reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): if the key doesn't exist * [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): a single, randomly selected field when the `count` option is not used * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list containing `count` fields when the `count` option is used, or an empty array if the key does not exists. * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of fields and their values when `count` and `WITHVALUES` were both used. -tab-sep- Any of the following: * [Null reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#nulls): if the key doesn't exist * [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): a single, randomly selected field when the `count` option is not used * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list containing `count` fields when the `count` option is used, or an empty array if the key does not exists. * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of fields and their values when `count` and `WITHVALUES` were both used. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
HSET coin heads obverse tails reverse edge null
HRANDFIELD coin
HRANDFIELD coin
HRANDFIELD coin -5 WITHVALUES
intro:
When called with just the `key` argument, return a random field from the hash value stored at `key`.

If the provided `count` argument is positive, return an array of **distinct fields**.
The array's length is either `count` or the hash's number of fields ([`HLEN`]()), whichever is lower.

If called with a negative `count`, the behavior changes and the command is allowed to return the **same field multiple times**.
In this case, the number of returned fields is the absolute value of the specified `count`.

The optional `WITHVALUES` modifier changes the reply so it includes the respective values of the randomly selected hash fields.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
When called with just the `key` argument, return a random field from the hash value stored at `key`.

If the provided `count` argument is positive, return an array of **distinct fields**.
The array's length is either `count` or the hash's number of fields ([`HLEN`]()), whichever is lower.

If called with a negative `count`, the behavior changes and the command is allowed to return the **same field multiple times**.
In this case, the number of returned fields is the absolute value of the specified `count`.

The optional `WITHVALUES` modifier changes the reply so it includes the respective values of the randomly selected hash fields.

sections.title: Examples
Specification of the behavior when count is passed
sections.content:
When the `count` argument is a positive value this command behaves as follows:

* No repeated fields are returned.
* If `count` is bigger than the number of fields in the hash, the command will only return the whole hash without additional fields.
* The order of fields in the reply is not truly random, so it is up to the client to shuffle them if needed.

When the `count` is a negative value, the behavior changes as follows:

* Repeating fields are possible.
* Exactly `count` fields, or an empty array if the hash is empty (non-existing key), are always returned.
* The order of fields in the reply is truly random.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="hrandfield-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

Any of the following:
* [Nil reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): if the key doesn't exist
* [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): a single, randomly selected field when the `count` option is not used
* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list containing `count` fields when the `count` option is used, or an empty array if the key does not exists.
* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of fields and their values when `count` and `WITHVALUES` were both used.

-tab-sep-

Any of the following:
* [Null reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#nulls): if the key doesn't exist
* [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): a single, randomly selected field when the `count` option is not used
* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list containing `count` fields when the `count` option is used, or an empty array if the key does not exists.
* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of fields and their values when `count` and `WITHVALUES` were both used.

{{< /multitabs >}}
